title: 字母袖扣谋杀案
summary:
Hercule Poirot confronts Dr. Ambrose Flavard regarding the deaths of Patrick and Frances Ives sixteen years prior. While the coroner originally ruled their deaths an accidental poisoning, Flavard admits he lied to protect the couple's reputations. He reveals that the pair committed suicide using digitalis (love-apple seed toxin) that Frances had stolen from Flavard's medical cabinet. Flavard explains his motive for the deception was to shield the couple from the harassment of villagers like Harriet Sipper and Ada Gransbury, whom he describes as "devils." He also confesses that his stubbornness in fighting these villagers contributed to the declining health and eventual death of Charles, the late husband of Margaret Ernst.

The ill Margaret Ernst reveals she remembers the contents of the suicide notes. She notes that Patrick’s note contained a message for Nancy Duane, a detail Flavard had withheld to maintain his lie. Margaret also reminds Flavard to disclose the threats made by Harriet Sipper, who had previously attempted to have the Ives' bodies exhumed from the churchyard on religious grounds.

Poirot decides not to expose Flavard's past lies to the authorities, allowing him to retain his medical license. Poirot also encourages Flavard to marry Margaret, noting their mutual love and their previous decision to remain apart out of guilt over Charles's death. After leaving the room, Poirot visits the graves of Patrick and Frances and finds the headstone lying in the mud, having been disturbed by Fred Krieger and Tobias Clayton. Poirot draws a flower in the dirt as a symbolic gesture of remembrance.
